headline: Chile first-baseline rotation assessment. Published score 62.5 (Established). Major in-window structural evidence: pension reform signed into law January 29 2025 by President Boric — mixed system raising pensions 14-35% for 2.8M seniors; gender-disparity suppression; recognizes paid and unpaid work; first major reform in decades. Constitutional reform process recognizing indigenous peoples started July 2025. December 2025 election: José Antonio Kast elected president — political shift. Chile grows with you universal program continues. ESCR Committee September 2025 raised concerns on indigenous land rights and safe drinking water. Net assessment: composite 65.0 vs published 62.5; +2.5 sub-threshold delta; Established band sustained.
key_evidence: January 29 2025: pension reform signed into law by Boric — mixed system, contributions 10% workers, 8.5% employers, plus state social-security component.
Pensions raised 14% to 35% for 2.8M senior citizens; gender disparity suppression; recognizes paid and unpaid work.
Reform increases maximum PGU from CLP 224,004 to 250,000 phased in by age cohorts (Sept 2025-2027).
Constitutional reform process started July 2025 recognizing all indigenous peoples in Chile.
Chile grows with you policy: universal education and health support; health system free of charge.
Plans underway to recognize care as fourth pillar of social protection.
December 2025: José Antonio Kast elected president — political shift; Boric term concluded.
September 2025: ESCR Committee raised questions on indigenous land rights and access to safe drinking water.

evidence.finding: Boric signs pension reform into law; major social-protection achievement.
New pension scheme structural detail; mixed contributions; effective September 2025; full implementation by 2035.

evidence.source: Government of Chile pension reform press
evidence.finding: Reform ensures women receive same pension as men with same age and savings.
ESCR Committee welcomed work-life balance efforts; raised concerns on indigenous land rights and safe drinking water.

evidence.source: Yale Law School human rights workshop
evidence.finding: Constitutional experiment under Boric; Kast rise (December 2025) — political shift.

scoring_rationale.AWR: 3.5 unchanged. SDG- and ESCR-aligned reporting structures.
scoring_rationale.ACT: 3.7 (+0.2). AC1 Responsiveness and AC4 Resource Mobilization: pension reform raising 2.8M seniors' pensions 14-35%; Chile grows with you universal program; care as fourth social-protection pillar — these are documented mobilization achievements.
scoring_rationale.EQU: 3.2 (+0.2). EQ1 Universality and EQ2 Priority for Vulnerable: gender-disparity suppression in pensions; constitutional recognition of indigenous peoples. Offset by ESCR-noted indigenous land-rights and water-access gaps. Net positive uplift.
scoring_rationale.BND: 4.0 unchanged. B3 Scope Clarity strongly evidenced via SDG-aligned planning and constitutional process transparency.
scoring_rationale.ACC: 3.5 unchanged. ESCR engagement with concerns acknowledged but not yet acted.
scoring_rationale.SYS: 3.8 (+0.3). S2 Long-Term Impact: pension reform fully implementing by 2035 — 10+ year horizon. S4 Structural Critique: constitutional reform recognizing indigenous peoples is structural-change action. S1 Root Cause Orientation: addresses pension system roots, not symptoms.
scoring_rationale.INT: 3.6 (+0.1). I1 Consistency: Boric administration delivered pension reform despite political compromise costs (the reform required compromise and bore real political cost). Modest uplift.

math_check.validator_check: +2.5 sub-threshold; Established band sustained. Within validator 2.0pt cycle ceiling at most dimensions.

negativeSignals: ESCR concerns on indigenous land rights and safe drinking water; political shift to Kast (December 2025) creates trajectory uncertainty; constitutional recognition of indigenous peoples is process-stage, not enacted.
positiveSignals: Pension reform delivered with gender-equity provisions; constitutional recognition of indigenous peoples in process; universal education/health (free of charge); care as fourth pillar of social protection; long-horizon implementation timeline.
sector_context: Chile is one of the strongest South American social-protection reform stories in the published index. Pension reform delivery is a major positive structural signal. Kast administration's posture toward continuing these reforms is a key next-cycle monitor.
open_actions_resolved: Constitutional reform process captured under SYS
ESCR concerns captured under ACC notation
open_actions_carried: Monitor Kast administration posture on pension reform implementation, indigenous rights, and social-protection programs
Re-queue for next rotation cycle when first Kast-administration policy actions take effect
